threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the Tube instead of 'the underground' or 'the line'—definitely a newbie marker.
Saw a guy on the 59 last night trying to sell homemade jam. Claimed it was 'the only food the late-night crowd needs'.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The way the kebab shop owner remembers your order like it's his secret recipe—definitely a sign you’ve settled in.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find a spontaneous dance party on a U-Bahn platform at 3 AM—complete with DJs and a crowd that knows how to vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell who’s local by their reaction to the BART delays; a sigh and a shrug, not a panic. It’s just part of the commute here.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo pull out a saxophone on the 19. He played for a few stops while we all just sat there, frozen in the moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always queue for entry at Parc Güell, but they miss the real charm at the lesser-known Plaça del Sol, where locals gather over drinks.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still call it ‘Sagrada Familia’ instead of just ‘La Sagrada.’ Plus, their camera is out for every corner. Classic signs.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You think a 5-minute walk means you’ll find a coffee shop. In Dalston, it means dodging three overpriced artisanal bakeries first.
Last night on the 59, a guy serenaded the whole bus with 90s R&B. By the time we hit Clapham, we were all clapping along like it was a concert.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw the guy in a three-piece suit ordering a döner at 2 AM. Only in Neukölln do fashion and late-night cravings blend so seamlessly.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find an U-Bahn station dedicated to just one club's supporters. The whole vibe shifts when game days roll around.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they're from here if they understand that a dog on the tracks isn't just an inconvenience, it's a new Facebook event in the making.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a whiff of something burning while waiting at the Civic Center stop—turned out to be a dog on the tracks. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: You can spot a tourist in Gràcia by their confusion at the small shops closing for siesta—it's not just a myth, folks.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by the way they still look for cafes with outdoor seating in winter, shivering while sipping their café con leche.
